sourceMovie.title: Let Me In
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Let Me In works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lost Boys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Let Me In clicked for you, The Lost Boys (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Let Me In?
faq.answer: Try The Lost Boys, Stake Land, Dracula—each shares DNA with Let Me In while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Let Me In?
faq.answer: Let Me In (2010) is best described as Drama, Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Let Me In worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
